title: The invisible engineering behind Lambda’s network
author: werner@allthingsdistributed.com (Dr. Werner Vogels)
summary: The subtle inventiveness that reduced cold start setup from seconds to 200μs.

title: S3 Files and the changing face of S3
author: werner@allthingsdistributed.com (Dr. Werner Vogels)
summary: Andy Warfield writes about the hard-won lessons dealing with data friction that lead to S3 Files

title: A chat with Byron Cook on automated reasoning and trust in AI systems
author: werner@allthingsdistributed.com (Dr. Werner Vogels)
summary: Over the past decade, Byron's team has proven the correctness of our authorization engine, our cryptographic implementations, and our virtualization layer. Now they're taking those same techniques and applying them to agentic systems.

title: Education is not one-size-fits-all for the latest CTO Fellows
author: werner@allthingsdistributed.com (Dr. Werner Vogels)
summary: Today, I’m honored to introduce you to the latest cohort of Now Go Build CTO Fellows. Individuals from around the world with diverse backgrounds (engineering, the humanities, business, healthcare, entrepreneurship, law) that have found their calling at the intersection of technology and education.

title: A little bit uncomfortable
author: werner@allthingsdistributed.com (Dr. Werner Vogels)
summary: Fear is actually a pretty good signal that you are pushing into the unknown, that real growth doesn’t happen without a bit of that associated discomfort, and that it’s worth becoming aware when it happens. Aware enough to consider actually leaning into it.

title: Tech predictions for 2026 and beyond
author: werner@allthingsdistributed.com (Dr. Werner Vogels)
summary: We’ve caught glimpses of a future that values autonomy, empathy, and individual expertise. Where interdisciplinary cooperation influences discovery and creation at an unrelenting pace. In the coming year, we will begin the transition into a new era of AI in the human loop, not the other way around. This cycle will create massive opportunities to solve problems that truly matter.

title: What is USSD (and who cares)?
author: werner@allthingsdistributed.com (Dr. Werner Vogels)
summary: While many of us chase the latest tech trends, innovative builders in Sub-Saharan Africa are leveraging a nearly 30-year-old messaging protocol to process hundreds of billions in transactions annually, reminding us that the best technology isn't always the shiniest, it's what actually solves customer problems.

title: Removing friction from Amazon SageMaker AI development
author: werner@allthingsdistributed.com (Dr. Werner Vogels)
summary: Building with Amazon SageMaker AI should be about innovation, not wrestling with development environments or building bespoke observability systems. Here's how we're removing roadblocks so builders can focus on what matters most.

title: Building with purpose: Stories from the Now Go Build CTO Fellows
author: werner@allthingsdistributed.com (Dr. Werner Vogels)
summary: This new five-part mini-series follows technology leaders from social impact organizations solving humanity's hardest problems - from crisis zones to community centers. Watch how they use drones to map disaster zones, AI/ML to predict food shortages, and open data to save lives.

title: A return to two-pizza culture
author: werner@allthingsdistributed.com (Dr. Werner Vogels)
summary: What made the Quick Desktop team successful is the same thing that has always produced the best work I've seen at Amazon: a small group of people who trusted each other, owned the problem end-to-end, and acted on their conviction.
